heading: Hospital Course:__page_no__2
text: hospital course: [patient_name] is a 91 y.o. female with a past medical history significant for anxiety, dementia, hypothyroidism, dysphagia, generalized weakness, gerd, hyperlipidemia, hypertension, encephalopathy, and recent uti as well as hip fracture 7 years ago presented to [facility_name]'s emergency department from [facility_name] nursing facility due to altered mental status. patient is largely obtunded at the time of my exam she is protecting her airway and will withdraw from pain however unable to participate in exam and anyway much of the information was gleaned from past medical records discussion with the er physician as well as daughter. daughter indicates that her mother has lived with her for the last 2 years until her hospitalization at [facility_name] she indicates that she was sent to [facility_name] hospital for possible uti for which she underwent antibiotic treatment and was sent to [facility_name] to complete antibiotic treatment and pt. daughter indicated that she is largely been challenged with mobility for the last 7 to 8 years ago since a hip fracture. she denies any other no changes in daily activity or medication that might of exacerbated that she states that reportedly her mother was awake alert and at her baseline yesterday. her baseline is alert and oriented by 2. er course initial labs significant for sodium 138, potassium 4.0, bun 26.0, creatinine 1.43, lactic acid 1.3, troponin 40, white blood cell count 8.0, hemoglobin 10.8 ua ordered however pending at this time abdominal ct indicates significant constipation enema ordered in the emergency room has not been given yet er discussed with daughter regarding code status i also personally discussed with daughter regarding code status she is confirmed dnr. patient will be admitted to a medsurg setting for further evaluation and treatment discussed at length with daughter over the phone they are within the differential is possible cva given the acute change in mental status will order head ct however given her age and comorbidities treatment will be limited. daughter verbalized understanding.
